visual_checks: Tab through interactive elements and observe whether each displays a clearly visible focus indicator
Check if the focus indicator has a contrasting outline of at least 2 CSS pixels on the perimeter
Measure or estimate whether the focus indicator color has at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors
Verify the focus indicator contrast between its focused and unfocused states is at least 3:1
Look for elements that use outline: none or outline: 0 without providing an equivalent custom focus style
Check if focus indicators are consistent across all interactive elements on the page
pass_conditions: All focusable elements have a visible focus indicator with at least a 2 CSS pixel solid outline around the full perimeter
The focus indicator has at least 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent unfocused colors
The change between focused and unfocused states has at least 3:1 contrast
Custom focus styles meet the minimum area requirement (at least as large as a 2px perimeter outline)
Focus indicators are not removed via CSS without providing an equivalent or better replacement
fail_conditions: Focus indicators are suppressed with outline: none and no alternative focus style is provided
Focus indicators are present but thinner than 2 CSS pixels
Focus indicator color has less than 3:1 contrast against the adjacent background
Some interactive elements show no visible change when focused
Focus indicator only appears on some sides of the element rather than the full perimeter
na_conditions: Page has no interactive elements that can receive keyboard focus
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Focus indicator colors could not be measured for contrast ratio calculation
common_mistakes: Using a light blue or light gray focus outline that fails contrast against a white background
Applying outline: none globally for aesthetics without adding a replacement focus style
Focus indicators that use only a color change (e.g., text color) without a visible outline or border
A 1px dotted outline that does not meet the 2px solid perimeter requirement
Focus indicators that meet contrast on light backgrounds but fail on dark-themed sections of the same page

examples.pass: All focusable elements display a 3px solid dark blue (#005A9C) outline on focus, providing well above 3:1 contrast against the white page background and clearly enclosing the full element perimeter
examples.fail: Buttons have outline: none in the CSS and no replacement focus style — when tabbing through the page, there is no visual indication of which element currently has focus
examples.partial: Most elements have a visible 2px focus ring, but several custom-styled dropdown triggers only show a subtle 1px light gray outline that fails the 3:1 contrast requirement against the white background

plain_meaning: When the focus indicator is visible, it must meet minimum size and contrast thresholds defined in the normative text: the indicator area must be at least as large as a 2 CSS pixel perimeter around the component, and must have at least 3:1 contrast between focused and unfocused states. This ensures the indicator is actually perceivable, not merely present.
scope_applies_to: The visual appearance of the focus indicator on focusable components
Thickness and area of outlines, borders, underlines, or fills used as focus indicators
Contrast between the focused and unfocused state of the indicator
Custom focus styling overriding browser defaults
scope_does_not_apply_to: Whether a focus indicator exists at all (that is SC 2.4.7)
Whether focus is obscured by overlays (that is SC 2.4.11 / 2.4.12)
Text contrast unrelated to focus (that is SC 1.4.3)
Controls that are not keyboard focusable
borderline_calibration: Do not flag under this SC when SC 2.4.7 is already failing; fix presence first.
Measurements require actual pixel-level evidence, not guesses.
Default browser outlines generally meet this SC; only flag custom styling that is demonstrably thinner or lower-contrast.
A visible but thin indicator is still a 2.4.7 pass and only a 2.4.13 concern.
